critical_files.why: Current node model still centers on file/scope/layer and lacks registry-first content_ref and structured RuleDescription fields.

critical_files.role: shared registry schema and default derivation
critical_files.why: Contains path-depth layer derivation, stable_id defaults, and identity_source logic that conflict with the locked explicit L0/L1/L2 semantics.
Primary place to lock legacy parsing, explicit level handling, stable_id-only identity, and future content_ref compatibility.
Currently reads full rule bodies from node.file, returns L0/L1/L2 payloads, and implements description-tier behavior that will be replaced by fab_get_rule_sections.
Registers fab_get_rules and exposes the old output shape that the refactor plans to remove.

Current implementation returns rule bundles and shared_entries; it must be redesigned around requirement_profile, description_index, required_stable_ids, ai_selectable_stable_ids, and selection_token.
Current zod schema only accepts paths/client_hash and exposes the old shared bundle payload.
Owns .fabric/audit.jsonl append/read logic and is the natural extension point for rule_selection telemetry.
Captures current always/path/description behavior and should become characterization coverage before replacing the API.
Current expectations encode shared bundle and description stub behavior; they are the starting point for Red-Green migration to neutral description-index output.
Best place to add rule_selection append/read assertions without coupling telemetry validation to tool wiring.
Will need fab_get_rule_sections registration and likely removal of fab_get_rules registration.
Watches only .fabric/agents.meta.json and .fabric/bootstrap/README.md today; registry-first content files or taxonomy/docs changes may require broader invalidation.
Currently proxies getRules for dashboard rule context and will drift if the MCP contract changes without an aligned HTTP story.
Current init writes bootstrap/meta/human-lock/forensic only; it is the implementation entry point for generating .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md.
Compiles .fabric/agents/**/*.md into agents.meta.json and derives layer from path depth; this must align with explicit registry-first levels and legacy import compatibility.
Useful to lock current stable_id/layer derivation and then migrate toward explicit level semantics.
Likely location for adding characterization and new assertions around INITIAL_TAXONOMY artifact creation.
Documents the current closed loop around fab_get_rules and fab_plan_context and must be updated alongside the refactor.
Still teaches fab_get_rules/fab_plan_context as the primary runtime tools.
Explains bootstrap and agent metadata lifecycle and will need alignment if init starts writing INITIAL_TAXONOMY and registry-first rule content.
Already documents the missing root .fabric runtime and current stable_id contract; useful as planning context and likely update target.

patterns.name: Registry metadata with derived defaults
patterns.notes: The current system derives layer/topology/stable_id from file paths. The refactor needs explicit semantic levels with legacy parsing retained only as compatibility input.

patterns.name: Description-tier stub delivery
patterns.notes: Description activation already exists, but it is string-only and still lives inside the old rule-loading model.

patterns.name: Tool schema mirrors service types
patterns.notes: Server tools expose zod schemas that closely track service return shapes, so protocol changes will cascade directly through both layers.

patterns.name: Audit logging is append-only JSONL with best-effort writes
patterns.notes: Existing get_rules and edit_intent events show the intended telemetry style for new rule_selection events.

patterns.name: Init scaffolding is centralized in one CLI plan/execution flow
patterns.notes: Adding INITIAL_TAXONOMY should go through buildInitFabricPlan and executeInitFabricPlan rather than ad hoc file writes elsewhere.

patterns.name: Current runtime root assumes bootstrap plus agents.meta plus optional agents tree
patterns.notes: The repository root currently lacks a complete .fabric runtime, so server-level tests should keep using isolated fixtures.

dependencies.notes: CLI registry compilation imports shared schema helpers and will inherit any schema break.
fab_get_rules appends compliance telemetry after rule delivery.
plan_context reuses get-rules loaders, matching, and payload builders; the new protocol may need a shared lower-level rule index layer instead.
Tool schema is thin and directly exposes the service output.
Removing or replacing the old tool requires coordinated registration and documentation changes.
Dashboard/API layer mirrors the old getRules service behavior.
Init establishes the scaffold and sync-meta establishes registry shape; taxonomy generation will likely need to share forensic/init facts with registry generation.
Schema changes in shared will propagate into server compile and runtime contracts.
CLI surfaces user-facing tool behavior and docs must stay aligned with server protocol changes.
New fab_get_rule_sections remains constrained by current MCP registration patterns.
All tool contracts and shared schema validation are zod-based.
Current path matching uses minimatch; requirement-profile and locality matching should not silently break existing glob semantics.

integration_points.impact: Add fab_get_rule_sections, redefine fab_plan_context, and remove or retire fab_get_rules.
Introduce structured descriptions and content_ref while preserving legacy file-based nodes during migration.
Add requirement_profile, description_index, selection_token, required/ai-selectable stable ids, and neutral diagnostics.

integration_points.surface: Edit-time section fetch and precedence merge
integration_points.impact: Replace full rule loading with section-based fetch that merges required_stable_ids with AI-selected L1 ids under fixed L2 > L1 > L0 precedence.
Append rule_selection events without interfering with unrelated local audit WIP.
Any contract change in MCP tools likely requires corresponding REST/read-model updates or explicit deprecation.
INITIAL_TAXONOMY generation and the new cognitive-alignment protocol must be documented alongside code changes.

conflict_areas.reason: Explicitly marked as unrelated WIP by the task. Relevant to telemetry design, but must not be overwritten during planning or future implementation.
Explicitly marked as unrelated WIP by the task and outside the cognitive-alignment refactor scope.
Explicitly marked as unrelated WIP. The locked protocol also says entity detection should stay optional and not depend on tree-sitter in the first version.
Likely to change together with sync-meta, server services, and tests; broad schema churn raises coordination risk across packages.
Legacy behavior is central to current MCP, HTTP, audit, and tests, so replacement will have broad cascading impact.
The TDD plan requires a major contract rewrite rather than a small extension.

constraints: Use the locked protocol inputs as authoritative: .fabric/rules/ for new rule bodies, stable_id-only identity, fab_get_rule_sections replacing fab_get_rules, and .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md as markdown-only.
Do not overwrite or clean unrelated WIP in .fabric/audit.jsonl, .intent-ledger.jsonl, or packages/cli/src/scanner/tree-sitter-probe.ts.
Treat the repository root .fabric directory as incomplete runtime state; favor isolated fixtures in tests instead of assuming the current repo is initialized.
Requirement Profile generation must stay deterministic and lightweight: path heuristics, intent keyword extraction, known_tech merge, optional detected_entities hints only.
RuleDescription must not define its own id. Identity remains at node stable_id.
fab_plan_context output must stay neutral for L1 selection: no score, confidence, match_reasons, negative_reasons, or matched_profile_fields in the returned description index.
selection_token storage is in-memory with TTL in the first version; invalid or expired token must be a deterministic hard error.
If ai_selected_stable_ids is non-empty, ai_selection_reasons must fully cover those ids; missing or invalid L1 selections are hard errors.
Missing requested sections must return empty section payloads with warning diagnostics and no fallback to full rule content.
Cross-layer precedence is fixed at L2 > L1 > L0; priority only sorts within the same layer.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 1: Characterize current init and registry behavior
recommended_task_slices.goal: Freeze current init outputs, stable_id derivation, and legacy agents.meta parsing before structural changes.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 2: Introduce shared schema for registry-first rule nodes
recommended_task_slices.goal: Add structured RuleDescription, content_ref compatibility, explicit level semantics, and stable_id-only identity in shared types and parsing.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 3: Add INITIAL_TAXONOMY artifact generation
recommended_task_slices.goal: Extend init scaffolding to write .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md from deterministic init/forensic facts.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 4: Refactor fab_plan_context to neutral description-index protocol
recommended_task_slices.goal: Replace rule bundle output with requirement_profile, description_index, required_stable_ids, ai_selectable_stable_ids, selection_policy, and selection_token.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 5: Implement selection-token cache and section fetch API
recommended_task_slices.goal: Add fab_get_rule_sections with token validation, AI-selected L1 merge, structured section parsing, missing-section warnings, and hard protocol errors.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 6: Append rule_selection telemetry
recommended_task_slices.goal: Extend audit-log service and tests to record rule_selection events in .fabric/audit.jsonl without changing unrelated working data.

recommended_task_slices.name: Slice 7: Align HTTP surfaces and docs
recommended_task_slices.goal: Update REST/read-only endpoints and the protocol docs so onboarding and internal specs match the new rule delivery model.
